rule.name: APIKit configuration is required for API implementation
rule.description: Application must include an APIKit configuration (<apikit:config>) to auto-generate the REST API implementation interface from the RAML/OAS specification. This ensures consistent API contract implementation and reduces manual coding errors.

rule.name: APIKit global exception handling strategy must be configured
rule.description: Application must define a global error handler with APIKit exception types (<mule:on-error-propagate type='APIKIT:*'>). This ensures standardized error responses and proper HTTP status code mapping for API errors.

rule.name: Configuration file exceeds maximum flow count limit
rule.description: Configuration files should contain fewer than 10 flows to maintain readability and modularity. Consider splitting large configuration files into multiple smaller files organized by domain or functionality.

rule.name: Configuration file exceeds maximum subflow count limit
rule.description: Configuration files should contain fewer than 5 subflows to maintain code organization and readability. Excessive subflows in a single file indicate the need to refactor into separate configuration files.

rule.name: Flow names must follow kebab-case naming convention
rule.description: Flow names must use lowercase kebab-case format (e.g., 'get-customer-details', 'process-order'). This pattern matches: lowercase letters, colons, curly braces, and hyphens. Consistent naming improves code readability and maintainability.

rule.name: Subflow names must follow kebab-case naming convention
rule.description: Subflow names must use lowercase kebab-case format (e.g., 'validate-input', 'transform-response'). This pattern matches: lowercase letters, colons, curly braces, and hyphens. Consistent naming conventions ensure code consistency across the project.

rule.name: Encryption key must not be exposed in log messages
rule.description: Logger components must not contain ${mule.key} in their message attribute. Logging encryption keys creates a critical security vulnerability by exposing sensitive credentials in log files. Remove any references to mule.key from logger messages.

rule.name: Set Variable doc:name must match the variable name
rule.description: The doc:name attribute of <set-variable> components should match the variableName attribute for consistency and clarity. This makes flow diagrams and debugging more intuitive by showing the actual variable name being set.

rule.name: Application must include a health check flow
rule.description: Application must contain at least one flow with 'healthcheck' in its name. Health check endpoints are essential for monitoring, load balancers, and orchestration platforms to verify application availability and readiness.

rule.name: Application must use configuration properties for external resources
rule.description: Application must include either <secure-properties:config> or <configuration-properties> to externalize configuration. This ensures credentials, URLs, and environment-specific settings are managed outside the code and can be changed without redeployment.

rule.name: Secure properties encryption key must use property placeholder
rule.description: The 'key' attribute in <secure-properties:config> must reference a property placeholder (e.g., ${encryption.key}) instead of a hardcoded value. Hardcoded encryption keys compromise security by exposing them in source code and version control.

rule.name: API autodiscovery configuration must be present
rule.description: Application must include exactly one <api-gateway:autodiscovery> element to register the API with Anypoint API Manager. Autodiscovery enables API analytics, policy enforcement, and centralized API governance.

rule.name: APIKit error handlers must define standard HTTP error responses
rule.description: Global error handler must include on-error-propagate elements for APIKIT:BAD_REQUEST (400), APIKIT:METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED (405), and APIKIT:NOT_FOUND (404). These error handlers ensure proper HTTP status codes are returned for common API validation errors.

rule.name: DataWeave payload transformations must use external .dwl files
rule.description: Transform Message components using <set-payload> should reference external .dwl files via the 'resource' attribute instead of inline scripts. External DataWeave files improve code organization, reusability, testing, and IDE support.

rule.name: DataWeave variable transformations must use external .dwl files
rule.description: Transform Message components using <set-variable> should reference external .dwl files via the 'resource' attribute instead of inline scripts. External DataWeave files improve code organization, reusability, testing, and IDE support.

rule.name: HTTP Listener must use HTTPS protocol for secure communication
rule.description: HTTP Listener configuration must specify protocol='HTTPS' in <listener-connection>. Using HTTP exposes data in transit to interception and tampering. HTTPS encrypts all communication between clients and the API.

rule.name: HTTP Listener port must use property placeholder
rule.description: HTTP Listener configuration must use ${https.port} or ${https.private.port} for the port attribute instead of hardcoded values. This allows port configuration to vary across environments (dev, test, prod) without code changes.

rule.name: Autodiscovery API ID must use property placeholder
rule.description: The 'apiId' attribute in <api-gateway:autodiscovery> must reference a property placeholder (e.g., ${api.id}) instead of a hardcoded value. This enables different API instances across environments to be managed independently in API Manager.

rule.name: MSSQL database host must use property placeholder
rule.description: The 'host' attribute in <db:mssql-connection> must reference a property placeholder (e.g., ${db.host}) instead of a hardcoded hostname or IP address. Hardcoded database hosts prevent environment portability and expose infrastructure details in code.

rule.name: Database connection URL must use property placeholder
rule.description: The 'url' attribute in <db:generic-connection> must reference a property placeholder (e.g., ${db.url}) instead of a hardcoded connection string. Hardcoded URLs prevent environment portability and may expose database credentials in code.

rule.name: JMS credentials must use property placeholders
rule.description: The 'username' and 'password' attributes in <jms:active-mq-connection> must reference property placeholders (e.g., ${jms.username}, ${jms.password}) instead of hardcoded values. Hardcoded credentials are a critical security vulnerability.

rule.name: SMTP configuration must use property placeholders
rule.description: The 'host', 'port', 'user', and 'password' attributes in <email:smtp-connection> must reference property placeholders (e.g., ${smtp.host}) instead of hardcoded values. This ensures email configuration can be changed per environment and credentials remain secure.

rule.name: HTTP Request must use HTTPS protocol for secure communication
rule.description: HTTP Request configuration must specify protocol='HTTPS' in <request-connection>. Using HTTP for outbound requests exposes data in transit to interception. HTTPS encrypts all communication with external services.

rule.name: HTTP Request port must use property placeholder
rule.description: HTTP Request configuration must use ${https.port} or ${https.private.port} for the port attribute instead of hardcoded values. This allows external service port configuration to vary across environments without code changes.

rule.name: HTTP Request must not configure Basic Authentication
rule.description: HTTP Request configuration should not include <http:basic-authentication> elements. This rule flags the presence of basic authentication configuration, which may indicate credentials are being managed in the configuration. Use more secure authentication methods or ensure credentials are properly externalized.

rule.name: HTTP Request Basic Authentication credentials must use property placeholders
rule.description: When Basic Authentication is used, the 'username' and 'password' attributes in <http:basic-authentication> must reference property placeholders (e.g., ${api.username}, ${api.password}) instead of hardcoded values. Hardcoded credentials are a critical security vulnerability.

rule.name: HTTP Request must configure TLS context for secure connections
rule.description: HTTP Request configuration must include a 'tlsContext' attribute in <request-connection> to specify SSL/TLS settings. TLS context configuration controls certificate validation, supported protocols, and cipher suites for secure HTTPS communication.

rule.name: HTTP Listener must configure TLS context for secure connections
rule.description: HTTP Listener configuration must include a 'tlsContext' attribute in <listener-connection> to specify SSL/TLS settings. TLS context defines the keystore, truststore, supported protocols, and cipher suites required for HTTPS endpoints. Missing TLS context prevents secure HTTPS communication.

rule.name: Application must include MUnit test configuration
rule.description: Application must contain at least one <munit:config> element indicating the presence of MUnit tests. Unit tests are essential for code quality, regression prevention, and continuous integration. Every Mule application should have corresponding test coverage.
